title: sempervivum marmoreum subsp. reginae-amaliae
intro: Sempervivum marmoreum subsp. reginae-amaliae, also known as Queen Amelia's houseleek, is a subspecies of succulent plant in the genus Sempervivum. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its high concentration of saponins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Queen Amelia's houseleek include its ability to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of the plant, including infusions and tinctures, have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, and it is also used as an ornamental plant due to its attractive, rosette-shaped foliage.
